9	type heartbeatStore interface {
10		TouchClaimHeartbeat(string) error
11	}
12	
13	// WithHeartbeat runs fn while periodically calling store.TouchClaimHeartbeat
14	// on a background goroutine at the given interval. The goroutine is stopped
15	// and waited for before WithHeartbeat returns.
16	func WithHeartbeat[T any](ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store heartbeatStore, fn func() (T, error)) (T, error) {
17		ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
18		defer ticker.Stop()
19		return withHeartbeatCh(ctx, beadID, ticker.C, store, fn)
20	}
21	
22	// withHeartbeatCh is the injectable variant used by tests to supply a stub tick channel.
23	func withHeartbeatCh[T any](ctx context.Context, beadID string, tickCh <-chan time.Time, store heartbeatStore, fn func() (T, error)) (T, error) {
24		hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
25		var wg sync.WaitGroup
26		wg.Add(1)
27		go func() {
28			defer wg.Done()
29			for {
30				select {
31				case <-hbCtx.Done():
32					return
33				case <-tickCh:
34					_ = store.TouchClaimHeartbeat(beadID)
35				}
36			}
37		}()
38		result, err := fn()
39		hbCancel()
40		wg.Wait()
41		return result, err
42	}

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/rand"
5		"encoding/hex"
6		"encoding/json"
7		"errors"
8		"fmt"
9		"net/http"
10		"os"
11		"path/filepath"
12		"sort"
13		"sync"
14		"time"
15	)
16	
17	// ADR-022 §Worker-server interface (rev 5). Workers are autonomous; the
18	// server's view of who's working is derived from best-effort event reports.
19	// This file implements the three ingestion endpoints:
20	//
21	//   POST /api/workers/register        — issue a worker_id for an identity envelope
22	//   POST /api/workers/{id}/event      — append a worker-emitted event
23	//   POST /api/workers/{id}/backfill   — replay buffered events after reconnect
24	//
25	// In-memory derived view backed by an append-only JSONL log at
26	// .ddx/server/worker-events.jsonl under the server's WorkingDir. Both the
27	// registry and the log are best-effort: if the server crashes the workers
28	// keep operating and re-register on next probe.
29	
30	const workerIngestMaxBodyBytes = 1 << 20 // 1 MiB cap on register/event/backfill payloads.
31	
32	// errUnknownWorker signals that a worker_id is not present in the in-memory
33	// registry. Surfaced as 410 Gone so the worker re-registers within the same
34	// probe cycle (ADR-022 §Probe + freshness state model).
35	var errUnknownWorker = errors.New("unknown_worker")
36	
37	// workerIdentity is the thin envelope POSTed on /api/workers/register.
38	type workerIdentity struct {
39		ProjectRoot  string    `json:"project_root"`
40		Harness      string    `json:"harness"`
41		Model        string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
42		ExecutorPID  int       `json:"executor_pid"`
43		ExecutorHost string    `json:"executor_host"`
44		StartedAt    time.Time `json:"started_at"`
45	}
46	
47	// workerEvent mirrors a single bead-event the worker would write to its
48	// local event log. The wire payload is intentionally minimal — the bead's
49	// local log remains the authoritative copy.
50	type workerEvent struct {
51		BeadID    string          `json:"bead_id"`
52		AttemptID string          `json:"attempt_id"`
53		Kind      string          `json:"kind"`
54		Body      json.RawMessage `json:"body,omitempty"`
55	}
56	
57	// workerBackfillRequest carries the worker's NotConnected ring buffer.
58	// dropped=true means oldest events were silently dropped at the worker
59	// (rev 5: HadDroppedBackfill flag surfaces "consult bead-local logs").
60	type workerBackfillRequest struct {
61		Events  []workerEvent `json:"events"`
62		Dropped bool          `json:"dropped,omitempty"`
63	}
64	
65	// workerRecord is the per-worker derived state the server holds in memory.
66	type workerRecord struct {
67		WorkerID            string         `json:"worker_id"`
68		Identity            workerIdentity `json:"identity"`
69		RegisteredAt        time.Time      `json:"registered_at"`
70		LastEventAt         time.Time      `json:"last_event_at"`
71		MirrorFailuresCount int            `json:"mirror_failures_count"`
72		HadDroppedBackfill  bool           `json:"had_dropped_backfill"`
73		CurrentBead         string         `json:"current_bead,omitempty"`
74		CurrentAttempt      string         `json:"current_attempt,omitempty"`
75	}
76	
77	// workerIngestRegistry holds the in-memory derived view + owns the
78	// append-only event log. All exported methods are safe for concurrent use.
79	type workerIngestRegistry struct {
80		mu       sync.Mutex
81		workers  map[string]*workerRecord
82		logPath  string
83		logFile  *os.File
84		openOnce sync.Once
85		openErr  error
86	}
87	
88	func newWorkerIngestRegistry(workingDir string) *workerIngestRegistry {
89		return &workerIngestRegistry{
90			workers: make(map[string]*workerRecord),
91			logPath: filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "server", "worker-events.jsonl"),
92		}
93	}
94	
95	// register stores a new worker record and returns the issued worker_id.
96	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) register(id workerIdentity) *workerRecord {
97		now := time.Now().UTC()
98		rec := &workerRecord{
99			WorkerID:     newWorkerID(),
100			Identity:     id,
101			RegisteredAt: now,
102			LastEventAt:  now,
103		}
104		r.mu.Lock()
105		r.workers[rec.WorkerID] = rec
106		r.mu.Unlock()
107		return rec
108	}
109	
110	// recordEvent appends ev to the JSONL log and refreshes the worker's
111	// last_event_at timestamp. Returns errUnknownWorker if workerID is not
112	// registered.
113	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) recordEvent(workerID string, ev workerEvent) error {
114		r.mu.Lock()
115		rec, ok := r.workers[workerID]
116		if !ok {
117			r.mu.Unlock()
118			return errUnknownWorker
119		}
120		rec.LastEventAt = time.Now().UTC()
121		if ev.BeadID != "" {
122			rec.CurrentBead = ev.BeadID
123		}
124		if ev.AttemptID != "" {
125			rec.CurrentAttempt = ev.AttemptID
126		}
127		r.mu.Unlock()
128		return r.append(workerID, ev)
129	}
130	
131	// recordBackfill appends every event in the buffer; flips
132	// HadDroppedBackfill when the worker reports dropped overflow.
133	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) recordBackfill(workerID string, req workerBackfillRequest) error {
134		r.mu.Lock()
135		rec, ok := r.workers[workerID]
136		if !ok {
137			r.mu.Unlock()
138			return errUnknownWorker
139		}
140		if req.Dropped {
141			rec.HadDroppedBackfill = true
142		}
143		if len(req.Events) > 0 {
144			rec.LastEventAt = time.Now().UTC()
145			last := req.Events[len(req.Events)-1]
146			if last.BeadID != "" {
147				rec.CurrentBead = last.BeadID
148			}
149			if last.AttemptID != "" {
150				rec.CurrentAttempt = last.AttemptID
151			}
152		}
153		r.mu.Unlock()
154		for _, ev := range req.Events {
155			if err := r.append(workerID, ev); err != nil {
156				return err
157			}
158		}
159		return nil
160	}
161	
162	// snapshot returns a copy of the registry for read-only inspection (tests
163	// and future GraphQL consumers). The returned slice is a stable copy; the
164	// records themselves are pointers into the registry and must be treated
165	// as read-only.
166	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) snapshot() []*workerRecord {
167		r.mu.Lock()
168		defer r.mu.Unlock()
169		out := make([]*workerRecord, 0, len(r.workers))
170		for _, rec := range r.workers {
171			copy := *rec
172			out = append(out, &copy)
173		}
174		return out
175	}
176	
177	// loggedEvent is the on-disk shape of a single line in worker-events.jsonl.
178	// Wraps the worker-supplied event with the receiving worker_id and the
179	// server's timestamp.
180	type loggedEvent struct {
181		WorkerID  string          `json:"worker_id"`
182		Timestamp time.Time       `json:"timestamp"`
183		BeadID    string          `json:"bead_id,omitempty"`
184		AttemptID string          `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
185		Kind      string          `json:"kind"`
186		Body      json.RawMessage `json:"body,omitempty"`
187	}
188	
189	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) append(workerID string, ev workerEvent) error {
190		r.mu.Lock()
191		defer r.mu.Unlock()
192		r.openOnce.Do(func() {
193			if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(r.logPath), 0o755); err != nil {
194				r.openErr = err
195				return
196			}
197			f, err := os.OpenFile(r.logPath, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
198			if err != nil {
199				r.openErr = err
200				return
201			}
202			r.logFile = f
203		})
204		if r.openErr != nil {
205			return r.openErr
206		}
207		line, err := json.Marshal(loggedEvent{
208			WorkerID:  workerID,
209			Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
210			BeadID:    ev.BeadID,
211			AttemptID: ev.AttemptID,
212			Kind:      ev.Kind,
213			Body:      ev.Body,
214		})
215		if err != nil {
216			return err
217		}
218		line = append(line, '\n')
219		_, err = r.logFile.Write(line)
220		return err
221	}
222	
223	func newWorkerID() string {
224		var b [12]byte
225		if _, err := rand.Read(b[:]); err != nil {
226			// crypto/rand failure is unrecoverable; fall back to a timestamp
227			// stub so the path is non-empty (registration still succeeds and
228			// the operator sees a malformed-looking ID in the UI).
229			return fmt.Sprintf("wkr-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
230		}
231		return "wkr-" + hex.EncodeToString(b[:])
232	}
233	
234	// --- HTTP handlers -----------------------------------------------------
235	
236	func (s *Server) handleWorkerRegister(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
237		var id workerIdentity
238		if err := decodeWorkerIngestBody(r, &id); err != nil {
239			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
240			return
241		}
242		if id.ProjectRoot == "" {
243			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "project_root required"})
244			return
245		}
246		rec := s.workerIngest.register(id)
247		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{"worker_id": rec.WorkerID})
248	}
249	
250	func (s *Server) handleWorkerEvent(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
251		workerID := r.PathValue("id")
252		if workerID == "" {
253			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "worker id required"})
254			return
255		}
256		var ev workerEvent
257		if err := decodeWorkerIngestBody(r, &ev); err != nil {
258			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
259			return
260		}
261		if ev.Kind == "" {
262			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "kind required"})
263			return
264		}
265		if err := s.workerIngest.recordEvent(workerID, ev); err != nil {
266			if errors.Is(err, errUnknownWorker) {
267				writeJSON(w, http.StatusGone, map[string]string{"error": "unknown_worker"})
268				return
269			}
270			writeJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
271			return
272		}
273		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
274	}
275	
276	func (s *Server) handleWorkerBackfill(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
277		workerID := r.PathValue("id")
278		if workerID == "" {
279			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "worker id required"})
280			return
281		}
282		var req workerBackfillRequest
283		if err := decodeWorkerIngestBody(r, &req); err != nil {
284			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
285			return
286		}
287		if err := s.workerIngest.recordBackfill(workerID, req); err != nil {
288			if errors.Is(err, errUnknownWorker) {
289				writeJSON(w, http.StatusGone, map[string]string{"error": "unknown_worker"})
290				return
291			}
292			writeJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
293			return
294		}
295		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
296	}
297	
298	// workerIngestView is the wire shape returned by GET /api/workers — a
299	// flattened snapshot of the runtime registry used by `legacy agent doctor`
300	// (ADR-022 step 6). The on-disk .ddx/workers/<id>/status.json layout
301	// remains the fallback source for one alpha release.
302	type workerIngestView struct {
303		WorkerID            string    `json:"worker_id"`
304		ProjectRoot         string    `json:"project_root"`
305		Harness             string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
306		Model               string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
307		RegisteredAt        time.Time `json:"registered_at"`
308		LastEventAt         time.Time `json:"last_event_at"`
309		MirrorFailuresCount int       `json:"mirror_failures_count"`
310		HadDroppedBackfill  bool      `json:"had_dropped_backfill,omitempty"`
311		Freshness           string    `json:"freshness"`
312	}
313	
314	// handleWorkerIngestList serves GET /api/workers: a snapshot of the
315	// in-memory runtime registry (ADR-022 rev 5). Empty list when no workers
316	// have registered. Fields mirror workerRecord plus a derived freshness
317	// classification.
318	func (s *Server) handleWorkerIngestList(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
319		snap := s.workerIngest.snapshot()
320		now := time.Now().UTC()
321		out := make([]workerIngestView, 0, len(snap))
322		for _, rec := range snap {
323			out = append(out, workerIngestView{
324				WorkerID:            rec.WorkerID,
325				ProjectRoot:         rec.Identity.ProjectRoot,
326				Harness:             rec.Identity.Harness,
327				Model:               rec.Identity.Model,
328				RegisteredAt:        rec.RegisteredAt,
329				LastEventAt:         rec.LastEventAt,
330				MirrorFailuresCount: rec.MirrorFailuresCount,
331				HadDroppedBackfill:  rec.HadDroppedBackfill,
332				Freshness:           freshnessState(rec, now),
333			})
334		}
335		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].RegisteredAt.After(out[j].RegisteredAt) })
336		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, out)
337	}
338	
339	func decodeWorkerIngestBody(r *http.Request, v any) error {
340		r.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(nil, r.Body, workerIngestMaxBodyBytes)
341		if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(v); err != nil {
342			return fmt.Errorf("invalid request body: %w", err)
343		}
344		return nil
345	}
346	
347	// freshnessState classifies a worker by recency of its last_event_at against
348	// the probe interval (ADR-022 rev 5: connected ≤2× probe; stale ≤10× probe;
349	// disconnected otherwise). Probe default is 30s, hard-coded here because the
350	// per-worker poll_interval is not part of the rev-5 register payload.
351	func freshnessState(rec *workerRecord, now time.Time) string {
352		const probeInterval = 30 * time.Second
353		age := now.Sub(rec.LastEventAt)
354		switch {
355		case age <= 2*probeInterval:
356			return "connected"
357		case age <= 10*probeInterval:
358			return "stale"
359		default:
360			return "disconnected"
361		}
362	}

16	// fakeServer is a minimal stand-in for the worker-ingest endpoints. Tests
17	// drive Reachable to flip the server's "presence" without tearing down the
18	// httptest.Server.
19	type fakeServer struct {
20		mu        sync.Mutex
21		reachable bool
22		workerID  string
23	
24		registered       int32
25		events           int32
26		backfills        int32
27		lastEvents       []Event
28		lastDropped      bool
29		failNextRegister bool
30	}
31	
32	func newFakeServer(workerID string) *fakeServer {
33		return &fakeServer{reachable: true, workerID: workerID}
34	}
35	
36	func (f *fakeServer) Handler() http.Handler {
37		mux := http.NewServeMux()
38		mux.HandleFunc("/api/workers/register", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
39			f.mu.Lock()
40			if !f.reachable {
41				f.mu.Unlock()
42				http.Error(w, "down", http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
43				return
44			}
45			if f.failNextRegister {
46				f.failNextRegister = false
47				f.mu.Unlock()
48				http.Error(w, "fail", http.StatusInternalServerError)
49				return
50			}
51			atomic.AddInt32(&f.registered, 1)
52			id := f.workerID
53			f.mu.Unlock()
54			_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{"worker_id": id})
55		})
56		mux.HandleFunc("/api/workers/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
57			f.mu.Lock()
58			reachable := f.reachable
59			f.mu.Unlock()
60			if !reachable {
61				http.Error(w, "down", http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
62				return
63			}
64			// Expect /api/workers/{id}/event or /backfill
65			switch {
66			case hasSuffix(r.URL.Path, "/event"):
67				var ev Event
68				if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&ev); err != nil {
69					http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
70					return
71				}
72				f.mu.Lock()
73				f.lastEvents = append(f.lastEvents, ev)
74				f.mu.Unlock()
75				atomic.AddInt32(&f.events, 1)
76				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
77			case hasSuffix(r.URL.Path, "/backfill"):
78				var req struct {
79					Events  []Event `json:"events"`
80					Dropped bool    `json:"dropped"`
81				}
82				if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
83					http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
84					return
85				}
86				f.mu.Lock()
87				f.lastEvents = append(f.lastEvents, req.Events...)
88				f.lastDropped = f.lastDropped || req.Dropped
89				f.mu.Unlock()
90				atomic.AddInt32(&f.backfills, 1)
91				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
92			default:
93				http.NotFound(w, r)
94			}
95		})
96		return mux
97	}

99	func hasSuffix(s, suf string) bool {
100		return len(s) >= len(suf) && s[len(s)-len(suf):] == suf
101	}

133	// TestWorker_ProbeImmediateFirst — the first probe must fire without waiting
134	// for BaseInterval, so registration with an already-running server happens
135	// promptly.
136	func TestWorker_ProbeImmediateFirst(t *testing.T) {
137		fs := newFakeServer("wkr-imm")
138		ts := startFakeServer(t, fs)
139	
140		p := newTestProbe(func() string { return ts.URL })
141		// Set BaseInterval very long so we ONLY observe the immediate first probe.
142		p.cfg.BaseInterval = 10 * time.Second
143		p.cfg.MaxInterval = 10 * time.Second
144	
145		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
146		defer cancel()
147		p.Start(ctx)
148		defer p.Stop()
149	
150		deadline := time.Now().Add(500 * time.Millisecond)
151		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
152			if p.Connected() && p.WorkerID() == "wkr-imm" {
153				return
154			}
155			time.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)
156		}
157		t.Fatalf("probe did not register immediately; connected=%v workerID=%q registered=%d",
158			p.Connected(), p.WorkerID(), atomic.LoadInt32(&fs.registered))

161	// TestWorker_ProbeJitter — successive intervals must vary within ±JitterPct
162	// of BaseInterval (and stay positive). We sample 30 intervals and require
163	// that they span a non-trivial range (proves jitter is applied) and stay
164	// within the [1-pct, 1+pct] envelope.
165	func TestWorker_ProbeJitter(t *testing.T) {
166		fs := newFakeServer("wkr-jit")
167		ts := startFakeServer(t, fs)
168	
169		p := newTestProbe(func() string { return ts.URL })
170		const base = 50 * time.Millisecond
171		const pct = 0.20
172		p.cfg.BaseInterval = base
173		p.cfg.MaxInterval = base
174		p.cfg.JitterPct = pct
175		p.cfg.Rand = rand.New(rand.NewSource(7))
176	
177		const samples = 30
178		intervals := make([]time.Duration, 0, samples)
179		var mu sync.Mutex
180		done := make(chan struct{})
181		p.OnProbe = func(connected bool, interval time.Duration) {
182			mu.Lock()
183			if len(intervals) < samples {
184				intervals = append(intervals, interval)
185				if len(intervals) == samples {
186					close(done)
187				}
188			}
189			mu.Unlock()
190		}
191	
192		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
193		defer cancel()
194		p.Start(ctx)
195		defer p.Stop()
196	
197		select {
198		case <-done:
199		case <-time.After(10 * time.Second):
200			t.Fatalf("did not collect %d intervals (got %d)", samples, len(intervals))
201		}
202	
203		mu.Lock()
204		defer mu.Unlock()
205		min := intervals[0]
206		max := intervals[0]
207		low := time.Duration(float64(base) * (1 - pct))
208		high := time.Duration(float64(base) * (1 + pct))
209		for _, d := range intervals {
210			if d < low || d > high {
211				t.Errorf("interval %v out of envelope [%v,%v]", d, low, high)
212			}
213			if d < min {
214				min = d
215			}
216			if d > max {
217				max = d
218			}
219		}
220		if max-min < base/10 {
221			t.Errorf("intervals show ≤10%% variance (min=%v max=%v); jitter not applied", min, max)
222		}
223	}

225	// TestWorker_BackfillOnReconnect — events recorded while NotConnected MUST
226	// be delivered via /backfill on the NotConnected→Connected transition.
227	func TestWorker_BackfillOnReconnect(t *testing.T) {
228		fs := newFakeServer("wkr-bf")
229		ts := startFakeServer(t, fs)
230	
231		// Start with the addr unreachable (AddrFunc returns "").
232		var addr atomic.Value
233		addr.Store("") // empty means no server
234		addrFunc := func() string {
235			if v := addr.Load(); v != nil {
236				return v.(string)
237			}
238			return ""
239		}
240		p := newTestProbe(addrFunc)
241	
242		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
243		defer cancel()
244		p.Start(ctx)
245		defer p.Stop()
246	
247		// Wait one probe cycle to ensure the goroutine ran the immediate first probe
248		// in NotConnected state.
249		time.Sleep(60 * time.Millisecond)
250		if p.Connected() {
251			t.Fatalf("probe should be NotConnected with empty addr")
252		}
253	
254		// Record events while disconnected — they should buffer.
255		p.RecordEvent(Event{BeadID: "ddx-aa", Kind: "attempt.started"})
256		p.RecordEvent(Event{BeadID: "ddx-aa", Kind: "result"})
257		if got := p.BufferLen(); got != 2 {
258			t.Fatalf("buffer len after 2 disconnected events: got %d, want 2", got)
259		}
260	
261		// Flip addr to point at the fake server.
262		addr.Store(ts.URL)
263	
264		deadline := time.Now().Add(2 * time.Second)
265		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
266			if atomic.LoadInt32(&fs.backfills) > 0 && p.BufferLen() == 0 {
267				break
268			}
269			time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
270		}
271		if atomic.LoadInt32(&fs.backfills) == 0 {
272			t.Fatalf("no backfill POST received")
273		}
274		if got := p.BufferLen(); got != 0 {
275			t.Errorf("ring buffer not cleared after successful backfill; got len=%d", got)
276		}
277		fs.mu.Lock()
278		got := len(fs.lastEvents)
279		fs.mu.Unlock()
280		if got != 2 {
281			t.Errorf("server received %d events via backfill, want 2", got)
282		}
283	}

285	// TestWorker_BackoffOnFailures — five consecutive probe failures MUST
286	// stretch the next interval to MaxInterval (backoff).
287	func TestWorker_BackoffOnFailures(t *testing.T) {
288		// AddrFunc returns "" forever => every probe tick fails.
289		p := newTestProbe(func() string { return "" })
290		p.cfg.BaseInterval = 10 * time.Millisecond
291		p.cfg.MaxInterval = 200 * time.Millisecond
292		p.cfg.JitterPct = 0 // deterministic
293		p.cfg.BackoffThreshold = 5
294	
295		var seen []time.Duration
296		var mu sync.Mutex
297		want := 7 // first 5 = base, then 2 = max
298		done := make(chan struct{})
299		p.OnProbe = func(connected bool, interval time.Duration) {
300			mu.Lock()
301			if len(seen) < want {
302				seen = append(seen, interval)
303				if len(seen) == want {
304					close(done)
305				}
306			}
307			mu.Unlock()
308		}
309	
310		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
311		defer cancel()
312		p.Start(ctx)
313		defer p.Stop()
314	
315		select {
316		case <-done:
317		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
318			t.Fatalf("did not collect %d intervals (got %d)", want, len(seen))
319		}
320	
321		mu.Lock()
322		defer mu.Unlock()
323		for i, d := range seen {
324			if i < 5 {
325				if d != 10*time.Millisecond {
326					t.Errorf("interval[%d]=%v, want base=10ms", i, d)
327				}
328			} else {
329				if d != 200*time.Millisecond {
330					t.Errorf("interval[%d]=%v, want max=200ms (backoff)", i, d)
331				}
332			}
333		}
334	}

27	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {

29		CleanupLog      io.Writer

31		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
32		CleanupInterval time.Duration

38		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
39		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
40		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
41		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
42		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
43		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
44		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
45		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
46		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
47		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
48		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
49		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
50		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
51		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
52		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
53		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
54		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
55		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
56		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
57		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
58		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
59		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
60		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
61		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
62		// PreClaimTimeout bounds the pre-claim readiness hooks. Zero means use the
63		// binary default so a hanging readiness check cannot park the worker
64		// forever.
65		PreClaimTimeout time.Duration
66		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
67		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
68		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed. The companion
69		// report is still appended for operator visibility, but the stop itself is
70		// classified through work.StopConditionBudget.
71		BudgetStop func() (work.StopDecision, ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
72		// BinaryRefreshCheck, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next
73		// bead. A true result means the caller has started a replacement worker
74		// with equivalent arguments and this loop should exit before claiming work.
75		// Errors are logged and fail open so update-probing never blocks the queue.
76		BinaryRefreshCheck func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)
77		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and

80		Mode         executeloop.Mode
81		IdleInterval time.Duration

87		WorkerID     string
88		ProjectRoot  string

90		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
91		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →

93		TargetBeadID string
